sec_article.generatedArticle: In a small coastal town, the local fishermen had long been divided. Old Captain Elias would often advocate for sustainable fishing, while younger sailors sought to exploit every reef for profit. Their bias against each other’s methods grew so strong that no compromise seemed possible. One evening, a storm stranded a pod of dolphins near the shore. Elias’s crew rushed to help, but the young sailors hesitated, seeing only a chance to exploit the situation for publicity. Elias, however, insisted on a rescue. Working together, they freed the dolphins. The event forced them to depict a different future—one where cooperation mattered more than pride. From that day, they learned to advocate for balance, letting go of old bias to find a true compromise.

meticulous.meaning: adj. 一丝不苟的;非常细致的;谨小慎微的
meticulous.links: meticulously, meticulousness, careful, thorough, detail-oriented, scrupulous
meticulous.note:
She kept meticulous records of every transaction.
The designer is meticulous about every detail.
His meticulous approach helped prevent costly mistakes.

pragmatic.meaning: adj. 务实的;实际的;实用主义的
pragmatic.links: pragmatically, pragmatism, pragmatist, practical, realistic, matter-of-fact
pragmatic.note:
We need a pragmatic solution to the budget problem.
Her approach to management is direct and pragmatic.
He is more pragmatic than idealistic when making decisions.

coherent.meaning: adj. 连贯的;条理清楚的;一致的;有凝聚力的
coherent.links: coherently, coherence, incoherent, cohesive, logical, consistent
coherent.note:
She gave a clear and coherent explanation of the plan.
After the accident, he was too shocked to be coherent.
The team needs a coherent strategy for the next stage.
His arguments were coherent and persuasive.

articulate.meaning: 形容词：表达清楚的；口才好的；动词：清楚表达
articulate.note:
He is an articulate speaker who can explain complex ideas simply.
She articulated her concerns during the meeting.

inevitable.meaning: 形容词：不可避免的；必然发生的
inevitable.note:
Change is inevitable in a growing organization.
It seemed inevitable that prices would rise.

empirical.meaning: 形容词：以经验为依据的；实证的
empirical.note:
The theory is supported by empirical evidence.
Researchers collected empirical data from hundreds of patients.

ambiguity.meaning: 名词：模棱两可；歧义；不明确
ambiguity.note:
The ambiguity in the contract caused a dispute.
Good writing reduces ambiguity and confusion.

deteriorate.note:
His health began to deteriorate after the accident.
The economic situation may deteriorate further.

indispensable.meaning: 形容词：必不可少的；不可或缺的
indispensable.note:
Good communication is indispensable to effective leadership.
This tool has become indispensable in our daily work.

unprecedented.meaning: adj. 空前的;前所未有的
unprecedented.note:
The country is experiencing unprecedented economic growth.
This level of rain is unprecedented in recent history.

sophisticated.meaning: 形容词：复杂精密的；见多识广的；高级的
sophisticated.note:
The software uses a sophisticated algorithm.
She has a sophisticated understanding of global markets.
The restaurant attracts sophisticated customers.

exacerbate.note:
The cold weather will only exacerbate his joint pain.
His aggressive comments exacerbated the tension.

plausible.meaning: 形容词：看似合理的；貌似可信的
plausible.note:
Her explanation sounded plausible at first.
The police considered several plausible theories.

discrepancy.note:
There is a discrepancy between the two reports.
The accountant noticed a small discrepancy in the figures.

resilient.meaning: adj. 有弹性的;能迅速恢复的;适应力强的
resilient.note:
She is a resilient girl who quickly recovers from setbacks.
The local economy proved resilient to the global crisis.

incentive.note:
The government offers tax incentives to small businesses.
There is little incentive for them to work harder.

consensus.note:
The committee reached a consensus after hours of discussion.
There is a broad consensus that reform is necessary.

subordinate.meaning: 形容词：下级的；次要的；名词：下属；动词：使从属于
subordinate.note:
The manager asked her subordinates for feedback.
Safety should never be subordinate to profit.
He subordinated personal ambition to the needs of the team.

undermine.meaning: 动词：削弱；破坏；逐渐损害
undermine.note:
Constant criticism can undermine confidence.
The scandal undermined public trust in the government.

reluctant.meaning: 形容词：不情愿的；勉强的
reluctant.note:
She was reluctant to discuss the problem.
He gave a reluctant apology after the meeting.

comprehensive.meaning: 形容词：全面的；综合的；详尽的
comprehensive.note:
The report provides a comprehensive analysis of the market.
Students need comprehensive support during the transition.

intricate.meaning: 形容词：错综复杂的；精细复杂的
intricate.note:
The necklace has an intricate design.
The case involves an intricate network of financial transactions.

viable.meaning: 形容词：可行的；能存活的
viable.note:
We need to find a viable alternative.
The project is not financially viable without additional funding.

profound.meaning: adj. 深奥的;渊博的;极度的
profound.note:
The book had a profound influence on my thinking.
He expressed profound sympathy for the victims.

impartial.meaning: 形容词：公正的；不偏不倚的
impartial.note:
Judges must remain impartial.
The investigation should be conducted by an impartial expert.

skeptical.meaning: 形容词：怀疑的；持怀疑态度的
skeptical.note:
Many scientists were skeptical of the claim.
She remained skeptical until she saw the evidence.

detrimental.note:
Smoking is detrimental to your health.
The policy may have a detrimental effect on small businesses.

substantial.meaning: 形容词：大量的；重大的；实质的
substantial.note:
The company made a substantial profit.
There is substantial evidence to support the theory.
They discussed substantial issues rather than minor details.

allocate.note:
The government will allocate more funds to education.
We need to allocate time for testing.

anticipate.meaning: 动词：预期；预料；期待；提前应对
anticipate.note:
We anticipate strong demand for the new product.
She anticipated the problem and prepared a backup plan.
The children anticipated the holiday with excitement.

consolidate.note:
The company wants to consolidate its position in the market.
Several departments were consolidated to reduce costs.

constrain.note:
Limited resources constrain our choices.
The rules constrained them to act cautiously.

contradict.note:
The witness's statement contradicts the evidence.
He did not dare to contradict his supervisor.

deliberate.meaning: 形容词：故意的；慎重的；动词：仔细考虑
deliberate.note:
The mistake was not accidental; it was deliberate.
She spoke in a slow, deliberate manner.
The jury deliberated for two days.

endorse.meaning: 动词：公开支持；认可；签署背书
endorse.note:
Several experts endorsed the proposal.
The celebrity agreed to endorse the product.
Please endorse the check before depositing it.

evaluate.note:
Teachers evaluate students' progress throughout the year.
We need to evaluate the risks before making a decision.

fluctuate.note:
Oil prices fluctuate wildly from day to day.
My weight fluctuates between 60 and 62 kilograms.

hierarchy.note:
She rose quickly through the corporate hierarchy.
There is a strict hierarchy in the military.

implement.meaning: 动词：实施；执行；名词：工具；器具
implement.note:
The school will implement a new policy next semester.
Farmers used simple implements to prepare the soil.

infer.note:
We can infer from the data that demand is increasing.
Do not infer too much from a single example.

manipulate.note:
She knows how to manipulate people to get what she wants.
He manipulated the clay into a bowl.

mitigate.note:
The new measures may mitigate the impact of inflation.
Planting trees can help mitigate climate change.

negotiate.note:
The union is trying to negotiate a new contract.
We managed to negotiate a better price.

perceive.note:
People perceive risk in different ways.
He perceived a change in her attitude.
The policy is perceived as unfair by many workers.

prevail.note:
A calm mood prevailed after the announcement.
Justice will prevail in the end.
Traditional customs still prevail in some villages.

reinforce.note:
The teacher used examples to reinforce the lesson.
The walls were reinforced with steel.
More troops were sent to reinforce the border.

speculate.note:
It is too early to speculate about the cause of the accident.
Some investors speculate in foreign currencies.

sustain.meaning: 动词：维持；支撑；遭受；证实
sustain.note:
The organization needs more funding to sustain its programs.
The bridge can sustain heavy traffic.
He sustained a serious injury during the game.
The evidence sustained her claim.

transform.note:
Technology has transformed the way we work.
The old factory was transformed into an art center.

validate.meaning: 动词：确认；证实；使合法有效
validate.note:
The experiment helped validate the hypothesis.
Please validate your ticket before boarding.
Her results were validated by an independent team.

versatile.meaning: 形容词：多才多艺的；用途广泛的
versatile.note:
She is a versatile actor who can play many roles.
This versatile tool can be used in several ways.

advocate.meaning: vt. 提倡;主张; n. 倡导者;拥护者
advocate.note:
Many experts advocate for reform in education.
She is a strong advocate of free trade.

compelling.meaning: 形容词：引人注目的；令人信服的；强制性的
compelling.note:
The documentary tells a compelling story.
The lawyer presented compelling evidence.
There is a compelling need for reform.

consecutive.meaning: 形容词：连续的；连贯发生的
consecutive.note:
The team won five consecutive games.
She worked for twelve consecutive hours.

constitute.meaning: 动词：构成；组成；被视为；设立
constitute.note:
Women constitute a large proportion of the workforce.
His actions constitute a serious violation of the rules.
The committee was constituted last year.

controversial.meaning: 形容词：有争议的；引起争论的
controversial.note:
The government introduced a controversial law.
The topic remains highly controversial among experts.

crucial.meaning: 形容词：至关重要的；关键的
crucial.note:
Good timing is crucial to success.
The next few days will be crucial for the negotiations.

deficient.meaning: 形容词：不足的；有缺陷的；缺乏的
deficient.note:
The diet is deficient in vitamin D.
The report was deficient in important details.

depict.note:
The movie depicts the hardships of war.
The paintings depict local landscapes.

derive.note:
Many medicines are derived from plants.
She derives great pleasure from music.
The formula can be derived from basic principles.

distort.note:
The mirror distorted his reflection.
The article distorted the facts.
Pain distorted her face.

enhance.note:
The new lighting will enhance the appearance of the room.
Training can enhance employee performance.

explicit.meaning: 形容词：明确的；清楚的；露骨的
explicit.note:
The instructions must be explicit.
She gave explicit permission to use the data.
The film contains explicit language.

facilitate.note:
The new system will facilitate communication between departments.
A mediator helped facilitate the discussion.

feasible.meaning: 形容词：可行的；可实现的
feasible.note:
The plan is technically feasible.
It is not feasible to finish the project in one week.

fundamental.meaning: 形容词：基本的；根本的；名词：基本原则
fundamental.note:
Trust is fundamental to any relationship.
The course teaches the fundamentals of programming.
The reform brought fundamental changes.

generate.note:
The campaign generated a lot of interest.
Solar panels generate electricity.
The report generated serious debate.

hypothesis.note:
Our findings support the original hypothesis.
He formulated a hypothesis to explain the phenomenon.

illustrate.meaning: 动词：说明；阐明；给……配插图
illustrate.note:
The chart illustrates the rise in sales.
This example illustrates the importance of planning.
The book was illustrated by a famous artist.

justify.meaning: vt. 证明...是正当的;替...辩护
justify.note:
How can you justify such high expenditure?
The results justify the costs.

legislate.note:
Governments should legislate to protect consumers.
It is difficult to legislate against every possible risk.

maximize.note:
The company aims to maximize profits.
Plan your schedule carefully to maximize productivity.

objective.meaning: 形容词：客观的；名词：目标
objective.note:
We need an objective assessment of the situation.
The main objective is to reduce costs.

obstacle.note:
Lack of funding is the main obstacle to our progress.
She overcame many obstacles to achieve her dream.

phenomenon.meaning: 名词：现象；非凡的人或事物
phenomenon.note:
Climate change is a global phenomenon.
The young singer became a cultural phenomenon.

prioritize.meaning: 动词：优先处理；按重要性排序
prioritize.note:
You need to prioritize your most important tasks.
The hospital must prioritize patients with urgent needs.

regulate.note:
The government regulates the banking industry.
The body regulates its temperature automatically.

relevant.meaning: 形容词：相关的；有意义的；切题的
relevant.note:
Please include only relevant information.
Her experience is highly relevant to the job.

restore.note:
The new policy aims to restore public confidence.
They restored the old building carefully.
The stolen painting was restored to its owner.

reveal.note:
The report revealed serious problems in the system.
She refused to reveal her source.
The clouds moved away to reveal the mountains.

rigid.meaning: 形容词：严格的；僵硬的；不灵活的
rigid.note:
The company has a rigid management structure.
His body became rigid with fear.
Rigid rules can limit creativity.

signify.meaning: 动词：表示；意味着；具有重要性
signify.note:
A red light signifies danger.
The agreement signifies a major step forward.
His silence may not signify approval.

simulate.note:
The program can simulate real-world conditions.
Pilots train in machines that simulate flight.
He tried to simulate confidence during the interview.

strategy.note:
The company needs a long-term strategy.
Her strategy helped the team win the competition.

subsequent.note:
Subsequent research confirmed the findings.
The decision had a major impact on subsequent events.

transparent.meaning: 形容词：透明的；公开透明的；易懂的
transparent.note:
The bottle is made of transparent glass.
The process must be fair and transparent.
Her explanation was simple and transparent.

trigger.meaning: 动词：引发；触发；名词：扳机；诱因
trigger.note:
The news triggered a sharp fall in stock prices.
Dust can trigger an allergic reaction.
He pulled the trigger without hesitation.
Stress is a common trigger for headaches.

ultimate.meaning: 形容词：最终的；根本的；极致的
ultimate.note:
Our ultimate goal is customer satisfaction.
The ultimate cause of the problem is poor planning.
This hotel offers the ultimate luxury experience.

utilize.note:
The company wants to utilize its resources more efficiently.
Students should utilize the library for research.

vulnerable.meaning: adj. 易受伤害的;脆弱的;有弱点的
vulnerable.note:
Older people are more vulnerable to the flu.
The city is vulnerable to sea-level rise.

withstand.note:
The building was designed to withstand strong earthquakes.
He could not withstand the pressure from his peers.

yield.meaning: vt. 产生;屈服;让步; n. 产量;收益
yield.note:
The investment yields high returns.
He refused to yield to their demands.

animal.note:
A dog is a loyal animal.
They went to the zoo to see wild animals.

beautiful.note:
What a beautiful sunset!
She sang a beautiful song.

climate.note:
This region has a warm climate.
The local climate is dry.

driver.note:
The bus driver was very polite.
He is a safe driver.

expensive.note:
That car is too expensive for me.
Eating out in this city is expensive.

health.note:
Exercise is good for your health.
He was restored to full health.

journey.note:
It was a long and tiring journey.
Have a safe journey!

market.meaning: n. 市场; vt. 营销
market.note:
I bought some fresh vegetables at the market.
They are trying to market their new product.

nature.note:
We should live in harmony with nature.
It is his nature to be quiet.

opinion.note:
In my opinion, this plan is perfect.
I would like to hear your opinion.

popular.note:
Soccer is a very popular sport.
She is the most popular girl in class.

science.note:
He is interested in science.
Science has changed our lives.

temperature.note:
The temperature dropped below freezing last night.
She has a high temperature.

useful.note:
This dictionary is very useful.
He gave me some useful advice.

village.note:
He grew up in a small fishing village.
The whole village was flooded.

ability.note:
She has the ability to manage this project.
His ability to speak four languages is impressive.

business.note:
She is in the fashion business.
None of your business.

challenge.meaning: n. 挑战; vt. 向...挑战
challenge.note:
Learning a new language is a great challenge.
She challenged me to a game of chess.

decision.note:
It was a difficult decision to make.
Have you come to a decision yet?

environment.note:
We must protect the environment.
A pleasant working environment is important.

future.note:
What are your plans for the future?
He has a bright future ahead of him.

government.note:
The government announced a tax cut.
He works for the local government.

history.note:
She is studying European history.
This church is rich in history.

improve.note:
I want to improve my speaking skills.
The weather is starting to improve.

knowledge.note:
He has a wide knowledge of history.
Knowledge is power.

method.note:
We need to find a better method.
This method has been proven effective.

necessary.note:
Is it necessary to sign the form now?
Take only what is necessary.

prevent.note:
Vaccines prevent diseases.
Nothing can prevent me from going.

reduce.note:
We must reduce our expenses.
The price was reduced by twenty percent.

society.note:
These changes will benefit society as a whole.
She is a member of the local historical society.

analysis.note:
The expert performed a detailed analysis of the market.
Further analysis is needed to confirm the results.

benefit.meaning: n. 利益;津贴; vt. 有益于; vi. 得益
benefit.note:
The new law will benefit low-income families.
He derived great benefit from the exercise.

consequence.note:
You must accept the consequences of your actions.
The error had serious consequences.

distribute.note:
The organizer distributed pamphlets to the crowd.
Profits are distributed among shareholders.

estimate.meaning: vt. 估计;估算; n. 估价
estimate.note:
It is difficult to estimate the cost of the project.
We accepted the contractor's estimate.

function.meaning: n. 功能;职责; vi. 起作用
function.note:
The main function of the liver is to filter blood.
The machine stopped functioning properly.

indicate.note:
A red sky at night often indicates good weather.
The arrow indicates the direction of flow.

lecture.meaning: n. 演讲;讲课; vi. 演讲;讲评
lecture.note:
He gave an interesting lecture on modern art.
She will lecture on biochemistry tomorrow.

maintain.note:
They managed to maintain their lead in the race.
The company maintains that its products are safe.

obtain.note:
Further information can be obtained from the office.
He obtained a degree in computer science.

policy.note:
The government is reviewing its economic policy.
Honesty is the best policy.

require.note:
This job requires a lot of patience.
Students are required to attend all lectures.

source.note:
The sun is our primary source of energy.
She refused to name her sources.

bias.meaning: n. 偏见;偏向; vt. 使有偏见
bias.note:
The report was accused of political bias.
We must prevent bias from affecting our decision.

compromise.meaning: n. 妥协;折中; vi. 妥协; vt. 危害
compromise.note:
After hours of discussion, we finally reached a compromise.
We cannot compromise on safety standards.

guarantee.meaning: vt. 保证;担保; n. 保证书;担保物
guarantee.note:
I guarantee that you will be satisfied with the service.
The watch comes with a one-year guarantee.

novelty.note:
The novelty of the new toy soon wore off.
Electric cars are no longer a novelty.

paradox.meaning: n. 悖论;自相矛盾的人或事
paradox.note:
It is a paradox that light can behave as both a wave and a particle.
He is a paradox: a quiet man who loves noisy parties.

reconcile.meaning: vt. 使和解;调和;使一致
reconcile.note:
It is difficult to reconcile these two opposing views.
They finally reconciled after a long argument.

subtle.meaning: adj. 微妙的;细微的;敏锐的
subtle.note:
There is a subtle difference between the two colors.
He gave a subtle hint that he was not pleased.

anomaly.note:
A temperature of 30 degrees in winter is a weather anomaly.
We are trying to explain this scientific anomaly.

cacophony.meaning: n. 嘈杂的喧嚣声;刺耳的杂音
cacophony.note:
The city streets were a cacophony of car horns and shouting.
A cacophony of protest greeted the politician's speech.

dichotomy.note:
There is a false dichotomy between science and art.
She analyzed the dichotomy of nature and nurture.

ephemeral.meaning: adj. 短暂的;瞬息即逝的
ephemeral.note:
Fame is often ephemeral and easily forgotten.
Spring flowers are beautiful but ephemeral.

garrulous.meaning: adj. 喋喋不休的;饶舌的
garrulous.note:
The garrulous old man talked to anyone who would listen.
She became quite garrulous after a few glasses of wine.

idiosyncrasy.note:
Wearing mismatched socks was one of his idiosyncrasies.
Every language has its own idiosyncrasies.

juxtapose.meaning: vt. 并置;并列以作对比
juxtapose.note:
The exhibition juxtaposes modern art with traditional sculptures.
By juxtaposing the two stories, the author highlights the contrast.

laconic.meaning: adj. 简洁的;言简意赅的;冷漠的
laconic.note:
He sent a laconic reply: 'No.'
His laconic manner made him seem distant.

malleable.meaning: adj. 有延展性的;易受影响的
malleable.note:
Gold is a highly malleable metal.
Young minds are malleable and easily influenced.

nefarious.note:
They conspired in a nefarious scheme to cheat the public.
His nefarious deeds were eventually exposed.

obfuscate.meaning: vt. 使模糊;使混乱;故意使人糊涂
obfuscate.note:
She tried to obfuscate the issue with irrelevant statistics.
Do not use jargon to obfuscate the truth.

quintessential.meaning: adj. 最典型的;最具代表性的
quintessential.note:
He is the quintessential British gentleman.
This cottage is the quintessential country home.

recalcitrant.meaning: adj. 顽抗的;不驯服的;难对付的
recalcitrant.note:
The recalcitrant horse refused to jump the fence.
Staff members were frustrated by recalcitrant students.

scintillating.meaning: adj. 闪烁的;才华横溢的;生动有趣的
scintillating.note:
She gave a scintillating performance on stage.
The conversation was scintillating and full of wit.

taciturn.meaning: adj. 沉默寡言的;不苟言笑的
taciturn.note:
He was a quiet, taciturn man who rarely spoke.
Her taciturn nature was often mistaken for coldness.

ubiquitous.meaning: adj. 无处不在的;普遍存在的
ubiquitous.note:
Smartphones have become ubiquitous in daily life.
Fast food chains are ubiquitous in this country.

vacillate.meaning: vi. 犹豫不决;摇摆;波动
vacillate.note:
He vacillated between the two job offers for weeks.
Her opinions vacillate according to who she is speaking to.

winnow.note:
The selection committee will winnow down the list of candidates.
We must winnow the truth from the rumors.

zealot.note:
He was labeled a religious zealot.
Only a zealot would agree with such extreme measures.

abundant.note:
The region is abundant in natural resources.
They had an abundant supply of food for the winter.

coincide.meaning: vi. 同时发生;相符;一致
coincide.note:
My holiday plans coincide with yours.
The interests of the two companies coincide.

diverse.meaning: adj. 各种各样的;不同的;多元的
diverse.note:
The student body is very culturally diverse.
We hold diverse opinions on this subject.

evidence.meaning: n. 证据;迹象; vt. 证明
evidence.note:
There is no evidence to support his claim.
The police are gathering evidence against the suspect.

fluency.note:
One of the requirements for the job is fluency in French.
She spoke with great fluency and confidence.

genuine.meaning: adj. 真实的;真诚的;真正的
genuine.note:
Is this a genuine leather jacket?
He showed a genuine interest in our project.

hostile.meaning: adj. 敌对的;敌方的;不友好的
hostile.note:
They received a hostile reception from the crowd.
The environment is extremely hostile to life.

influence.meaning: n. 影响;势力; vt. 影响
influence.note:
My parents had a major influence on my career choice.
Don't let him influence your decision.

jealous.note:
She was jealous of her sister's success.
He gets jealous when she talks to other men.

limit.meaning: n. 限制;限度; vt. 限制;限定
limit.note:
There is a strict speed limit here.
We must limit the number of attendees to fifty.

motivate.meaning: vt. 激发;刺激;作为...的动机
motivate.note:
How do you motivate your staff to work harder?
Fear of failure can motivate us to try harder.

notion.note:
He has a vague notion of what he wants to do.

predict.links: predicts, predicted, predicting, prediction, predictable, unpredictable
predict.note:
It is difficult to predict the future accurately.
Scientists predict that the volcano will erupt soon.

quote.meaning: vt. 引用;引述;报价; n. 引文;报价
quote.note:
Can you quote a price for this repair work?
He quoted a passage from Shakespeare.

reject.note:
The publisher rejected his new novel.
She rejected his offer of marriage.

secure.meaning: adj. 安全的;可靠的; vt. 获得;保护
secure.note:
Please make sure your seatbelt is secure.
He managed to secure a loan from the bank.

tolerate.links: tolerates, tolerated, tolerating, tolerance, tolerant, tolerable
tolerate.note:
I cannot tolerate this noisy environment any longer.
We must learn to tolerate different opinions.

urgent.note:
This is an urgent matter that needs immediate attention.
We received an urgent message from headquarters.

vivid.meaning: adj. 生动的;鲜艳的;清晰的
vivid.note:
He gave a vivid description of his journey.
I have a vivid memory of my first day at school.

wonder.meaning: vi. 想知道;感到惊讶; n. 惊奇;奇迹
wonder.note:
I wonder why he didn't show up.
It is a wonder that he survived the crash.

youth.note:
He spent his youth traveling the world.
She was a famous athlete in her youth.

zone.note:
This is a smoke-free zone.
They divided the city into different commercial zones.

xenophobia.meaning: n. 惧外;排外;仇外心理
xenophobia.note:
The rise of nationalism has led to increased xenophobia.
We must work together to combat racism and xenophobia.

trivial.meaning: adj. 微不足道的;琐碎的;无价值的
trivial.note:
Don't waste your time on such trivial matters.
It seemed a trivial objection at the time.

scrutinize.note:
The bank will scrutinize your financial records before approving the loan.
Every detail of the contract was closely scrutinized.

redundant.meaning: adj. 多余的;过剩的;被解雇的
redundant.note:
The computer system has redundant servers in case of failure.
He was made redundant after the company downsized.

prerequisite.meaning: n. 先决条件;前提; adj. 必备的
prerequisite.note:
A high score on the test is a prerequisite for admission.
This course is a prerequisite for advanced chemistry.

paradigm.note:
The study represents a major paradigm shift in physics.
He is considered a paradigm of professional integrity.

obsolete.meaning: adj. 淘汰的;废弃的;过时的
obsolete.note:
New technology has made the old machines obsolete.
Gas lamps became obsolete after the invention of electric lights.

lucrative.meaning: adj. 获利丰厚的;赚大钱的
lucrative.note:
He runs a very lucrative export business.
The merger proved to be highly lucrative for both companies.

jeopardize.note:
His careless actions could jeopardize the whole mission.
Do not jeopardize your health by working too hard.

inherent.meaning: adj. 固有的;内在的;与生俱来的
inherent.note:
There is an inherent risk in any financial investment.
Love of freedom is inherent in human nature.

grandiose.meaning: adj. 宏伟的;华而不实的;浮夸的
grandiose.note:
He has grandiose plans for starting a multinational company.
They live in a grandiose mansion with thirty rooms.

formidable.meaning: adj. 可怕的;难对付的;令人敬畏的
formidable.note:
The champion proved to be a formidable opponent.
They faced a formidable task of rebuilding the city.

diversity.note:
The biology class studied the diversity of life on Earth.
There is a great diversity of opinions on this issue.

cognitive.note:
We studied the cognitive development of young children.
Her cognitive skills remained sharp even in her nineties.

bureaucracy.meaning: n. 官僚主义;官僚机构
bureaucracy.note:
We must cut through the government bureaucracy to get things done.
Many businesses complain about excessive bureaucracy.

alleviate.note:
A warm bath can help alleviate muscle tension.
The government introduced measures to alleviate poverty.

benevolent.note:
A benevolent neighbor offered to look after our dog.
The organization was founded by a benevolent businessman.

aesthetic.meaning: adj. 美学的;审美的; n. 审美观
aesthetic.note:
The building was designed with both practical and aesthetic considerations.
She has a highly developed aesthetic sense.

synthesize.note:
He tried to synthesize the results of several different studies.
This laboratory can synthesize new compounds.

opulent.meaning: adj. 豪华的;奢侈的;富裕的
opulent.note:
They lived an opulent lifestyle in a luxury penthouse.
The hotel is decorated in an opulent style.

metamorphosis.note:
A caterpillar undergoes metamorphosis to become a butterfly.
The company went through a complete metamorphosis under the new CEO.

gregarious.meaning: adj. 社交的;群居的;爱交际的
gregarious.note:
She is a gregarious person who loves going to parties.
Sheep are highly gregarious animals.

esoteric.meaning: adj. 秘传的;深奥难懂的;限于少数人的
esoteric.note:
The specialist published a paper on an esoteric aspect of physics.
He has an esoteric interest in ancient languages.

dilatory.note:
The government was criticized for its dilatory response to the crisis.
His dilatory tactics delayed the progress of the negotiations.

connoisseur.note:
He is a connoisseur of fine French wines.
She is an art connoisseur who works for a gallery.

belligerent.meaning: adj. 好战的;交战的;好斗的; n. 交战国
belligerent.note:
The country adopted a belligerent foreign policy.
His belligerent attitude led to a fight.

acquiesce.meaning: vi. 默许;默认;勉强同意
acquiesce.note:
She chose to acquiesce in her husband's decision.
We cannot acquiesce to their unfair demands.

capricious.meaning: adj. 反复无常的;善变的;任性的
capricious.note:
The weather in this area is notoriously capricious.
She was guided by capricious whims rather than reason.

dogmatic.meaning: adj. 武断的;教条的;独断的
dogmatic.note:
He was so dogmatic that he refused to listen to anyone else.
She rejected his dogmatic assertions about religion.

equivocal.meaning: adj. 模棱两可的;可疑的;含糊的
equivocal.note:
His political record is somewhat equivocal.
He gave an equivocal answer that satisfied no one.

fastidious.meaning: adj. 挑剔的;过分讲究的;苛求的
fastidious.note:
She is fastidious about her personal appearance.
He is a fastidious planner who leaves nothing to chance.

iconoclast.meaning: n. 特立独行的人;反传统的人;打破旧习者
iconoclast.note:
As an iconoclast, he challenged all established artistic rules.
She was seen as an iconoclast in the field of politics.

loquacious.meaning: adj. 多话的;话多的;喋喋不休的
loquacious.note:
The loquacious host kept the guests entertained for hours.
He became quite loquacious after drinking a glass of wine.

obsequious.meaning: adj. 谄媚的;奉承的;卑躬屈膝的
obsequious.note:
The manager was surrounded by obsequious assistants.
He resented their obsequious behavior towards the boss.

perfidious.meaning: adj. 背信弃义的;不忠实的
perfidious.note:
We were betrayed by a perfidous ally.
He condemned their perfidious behavior.

superfluous.meaning: adj. 多余的;过剩的;累赘的
superfluous.note:
The writer cut several superfluous paragraphs from the article.
Your explanation is helpful but superfluous.

vociferous.meaning: adj. 大声疾呼的;喧嚷的
vociferous.note:
He was a vociferous critic of the government's economic policies.
The proposal met with vociferous opposition.

cacophonous.meaning: adj. 刺耳的；嘈杂刺耳的；不和谐的
cacophonous.note:
The street was filled with the cacophonous sound of horns and sirens.
A cacophonous mix of drums, whistles, and shouting came from the parade.
The meeting became cacophonous as everyone tried to speak at once.
cacophonous.links: cacophony, cacophonically, dissonant, discordant, harsh, jarring, euphonious

anomalous.meaning: adj. 异常的;反常的;不规则的
anomalous.note:
The scientists found an anomalous result in the experiment.
Her rapid recovery was anomalous compared with other patients.
The system flagged the anomalous transaction for review.
anomalous.links: anomaly, anomalously, anomalistic, abnormal, unusual, irregular

obsolescence.meaning: n. 过时; 淘汰; 废弃; 陈旧化
obsolescence.note:
Rapid technological change can lead to the obsolescence of older devices.
The company designs products carefully to avoid early obsolescence.
Planned obsolescence encourages consumers to replace items more often.
obsolescence.links: obsolete, obsolescent, planned obsolescence, become obsolete, obsoleteness

disability.meaning: n. 残疾; 障碍; 无能力; 不利条件
disability.note:
The company provides better support for employees with a disability.
His hearing disability has never stopped him from enjoying music.
A lack of formal education can be a disability in the job market.
disability.links: disabled, disable, ability, inability, accessibility, impairment, handicap

usefully.meaning: adv. 有用地；有效地；有益地
usefully.note:
The guide usefully explains how to set up the software.
We can usefully divide the discussion into three parts.
Her experience was usefully applied to the new project.
usefully.links: useful, useless, use, usefulness, uselessly, usable, used

vulnerably.meaning: adv. 脆弱地；易受伤害地；易受攻击地；坦率而显露脆弱地
vulnerably.note:
The child stood vulnerably in the cold without a coat.
The server was configured vulnerably and could be attacked easily.
She spoke vulnerably about her fears and past mistakes.
vulnerably.links: vulnerable, vulnerability, invulnerable, invulnerability, vulnerableness, invulnerably

vulnerability.meaning: n. 脆弱性；易受伤害；弱点；漏洞
vulnerability.note:
The report highlights the vulnerability of small businesses during an economic crisis.
Hackers exploited a vulnerability in the company’s payment system.
Children in poverty often face greater vulnerability to illness and poor education.
Showing vulnerability can help build trust in a close relationship.
vulnerability.links: vulnerable, vulnerably, invulnerability, invulnerable, weakness, exposure, susceptibility, security flaw, invulnerably

invulnerability.meaning: n. 不受伤害；无懈可击；刀枪不入；不易受影响
invulnerability.note:
The superhero's invulnerability made him almost impossible to defeat.
The company lost its sense of invulnerability after the data breach.
His calm attitude gave an impression of emotional invulnerability.

invulnerably.meaning: adv. 不会受伤害地; 无懈可击地; 不受影响地
invulnerably.note:
The fortress stood invulnerably against repeated attacks.
She argued invulnerably, leaving no room for doubt.
He seemed to move invulnerably through criticism and failure.

apple.note:
He is eating a red apple.
An apple a day keeps the doctor away.

book.note:
I read a book every week.
You need to book your flight in advance.

cat.note:
The cat is sleeping on the sofa.
She has a black cat.

water.note:
Please give me a glass of water.
I water the flowers every morning.

happy.note:
He looks very happy today.
Happy birthday to you!

house.note:
They live in a big house.
This house was built in 1990.

school.note:
Children go to school on weekdays.
He is the headmaster of the school.

friend.note:
She is my best friend.
They have been friends for years.

family.note:
I love my family very much.
We are a happy family.

learn.note:
I want to learn English.
She is learning how to drive.

father.note:
My father is a doctor.
He looks just like his father.

mother.note:
Her mother cooked a delicious dinner.
She became a mother of two.

milk.meaning: n. 牛奶; v. 挤奶
milk.note:
I drink milk every morning.
He is milking the cows.

bread.note:
Would you like some bread?
They buy fresh bread daily.

sleep.meaning: v. 睡觉; n. 睡眠
sleep.note:
I need to sleep early tonight.
He is fast asleep.
